variant_explanation: Sentence-BERT embeddings + LogisticRegression, NO metadata features. Originally trained as sbert_classifier_no_meta.joblib. The 'no_meta' variant was chosen over 'with_meta' after SHAP analysis (notebook 09) showed the with-metadata model classified by source-type instead of content (proxy concentration 27.6%). Validation macro-F1 dropped slightly (0.765 → 0.750) but the model now classifies by content, which is robust to new sources.

training_data.split: stratified train/val (~85/15 of the 1109 modelled rows); no held-out test set in the src pipeline
training_data.note: The artifact has 6 classes, not 8. The two human-only newsletter sections (update_from_pi, update_from_programme) survive cleaning (part of the 1654-row processed set) but are dropped before modelling, so the model trains on the 1109-row data/modelling/{train,val}.csv 6-class set.

metrics.evaluation_notes: Real-world eval on 264 manually-labelled articles from atlas-ed-data inference output (England, weeks 1-13 2026)

notes: First production model. Sentence-transformer (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) embeddings + sklearn LogisticRegression, no metadata features (per SHAP-driven decision in notebook 09 — metadata version caused source-type proxy classification). See docs/decisions_made_next_steps.md for full context.
